design_philosophy.core_loop: Vertically scroll through military terrain, destroy waves of enemy vehicles (tanks, battleships, aircraft, artillery), manage limited bombs, collect power-ups and score bonuses, reach runway checkpoints to progress.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (3 bombs per stage + collectible B-icons) vs. screen-filling enemy fire; pattern recognition and positioning to avoid projectiles while maintaining offensive pressure.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pilot a biplane through a war-torn military landscape inspired by Apocalypse Now; experience visceral stress relief through bomb deployment; accumulate knowledge and tricks to master increasingly difficult loops.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately structured for players to 'clear it via tricks and knowledge accumulated through gameplay.' Bomb mechanic prioritizes stress relief over traditional firepower progression. Checkpoint-based lives system softens early-stage difficulty to onboard beginners, then escalates aggressively in later loops. Military aesthetic tied to emotional war cinema rather than pure spectacle.

difficulty.lives_system: Three starting lives; bonus lives awarded at 50,000 points and thereafter. Hitting enemy fire costs one life and resets firepower to base state.
difficulty.continues_system: Arcade credit-based continuation system; players can insert coins to resume after game over.
difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoint system allows respawn at beginning of last reached checkpoint upon death; checkpoints placed at runway landings between stages.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Second loop and beyond increase bullet speed and enemy density. First stage tuned for beginners; difficulty scales per loop. Bullet speed mechanics employ overflow wrap mechanics to maintain intended difficulty curve. Players can manipulate scoring via runway landings (multiply bombs by 3000 points) for strategic progression.

progression.upgrade_system: Firepower levels via 'S' power-ups dropped by enemy planes; bomb stockpile increased by 'B' icons dropped from ground enemies; extra lives via score thresholds (50,000 points initially).
progression.unlock_mechanism: Five increasingly difficult stages unlock sequentially; runway checkpoints gate progression within stages. Second loop unlocks after completing stage five, with escalated enemy patterns and bullet speeds.
replayability_hooks: Second loop and subsequent loops increase in difficulty with faster bullets and denser enemy waves
Score-based bonus lives encourage high-score chasing and trick execution
Runway landing mechanic (3000 point multiplier per bomb stock) creates strategic scoring paths
Knowledge accumulation through repeated playthroughs reveals enemy patterns and optimal positioning

world_building_techniques: Military-themed enemy roster (tanks, battleships, aircraft, artillery) establishes war-zone setting
Scrolling backgrounds shift left-right to immerse players in Thailand-inspired scenery
Apocalypse Now aesthetic communicates emotional gravitas of warfare rather than abstract conflict
Biplane protagonist reinforces vintage military aviation fantasy

faction_indicators: Military forces (player biplane vs. enemy ground/air assets)
Geopolitical setting implied by Thailand-inspired environments
danger_signals: Constant enemy fire requiring reactive dodging
Escalating bullet speed in second loop and beyond
Firepower loss penalty on hit incentivizes damage avoidance
environmental_storytelling: Ground-based military installations (tanks, artillery, battleships) indicate active conflict zones
Runway landings suggest supply lines and forward operating bases
Scrolling directional backgrounds (left-right variation) create sense of spatial movement through contested territory

sources_used: wikipedia: Flying Shark article (gameplay, development philosophy, difficulty mechanics, progression structure)
